questions.question: I saw her ID card. _The smart lady must be forty._
options: 那个时髦的女士肯定是 40 岁左右。
那个聪明的女士肯定四十岁了。
这个时髦的女士可能是 40 岁左右。
那个时髦的女士不可能是 40 岁左右。
questions.correctAnswer: 那个时髦的女士肯定是 40 岁左右。
questions.explanation: 'must be' 表示非常肯定的推测（肯定是），'smart' 在这里表示'时髦的'，这是中考常考的词义辨析哦！
questions.memoryTip: 'Must' 就像是一个 100% 确定的侦探！'Smart' 不仅大脑聪明，穿搭也可以很'聪明'（时髦）！
questions.word: The smart lady must be forty.
questions.meaning: 那个时髦的女士肯定是 40 岁左右。
questions.example: I saw her ID; the smart lady must be forty.
questions.exampleTranslation: 我看了她的身份证；那个时髦的女士肯定是 40 岁左右。

questions.explanation: 注意首字母大写和句末标点，'must be' 后面直接跟年龄。
questions.memoryTip: 记住这三个关键：smart（时髦的），must be（肯定是），forty（40岁）。
questions.word: The smart lady must be forty.
questions.meaning: 那个时髦的女士肯定是 40 岁左右。

questions.memoryTip: 看到身份证（ID）了，所以推测语气要用最强的 must！

questions.question: I heard about his job. _Your father is a teacher, isn’t he?_
options: 你爸爸是一名老师，是吗/对吗?
你爸爸不是一名老师，是吗/对吗?
你爸爸曾经是一名老师，对吗?
你爸爸是一名老师，不是吗?
questions.correctAnswer: 你爸爸是一名老师，是吗/对吗?
questions.explanation: 反意疑问句中，'前肯后否'翻译成中文时，通常直接翻译为'是吗/对吗'来确认前面的事实。
questions.memoryTip: 反意疑问句就像一块磁铁，前面是正极（is），后面就是负极（isn’t），互相吸引！
questions.word: Your father is a teacher, isn’t he?
questions.meaning: 你爸爸是一名老师，是吗/对吗?
questions.example: Yes, he is.

questions.question: 你爸爸是一名老师，是吗/对吗?: ___
questions.correctAnswer: Your father is a teacher, isn’t he?
questions.explanation: 前面是肯定句 is，后面就要用否定形式 isn’t，这是典型的'前肯后否'结构。
questions.memoryTip: 先说事实 'Your father is a teacher'，再加个小尾巴 'isn’t he?'
questions.word: Your father is a teacher, isn’t he?
questions.meaning: 你爸爸是一名老师，是吗/对吗?
questions.example: Yes, he is.
questions.exampleTranslation: 是的，他是。

questions.question: 是的，他是。
questions.explanation: 对于 is 提问的疑问句，回答时如果是肯定的事实，就用 Yes 加上 is。
questions.memoryTip: 英语回答看事实！只要他真的是老师，就大声说 Yes, he is!
questions.word: Your father is a teacher, isn’t he?
questions.meaning: 你爸爸是一名老师，是吗/对吗?
questions.example: Yes, he is.
questions.exampleTranslation: 是的，他是。

questions.meaning: 你爸爸不是一名老师，是吗/对吗?

questions.question: 你爸爸不是一名老师，是吗/对吗?: ___
questions.correctAnswer: Your father isn’t a teacher, is he?
questions.explanation: 前面是否定句 isn’t，后面就要用肯定形式 is，这就是'前否后肯'。
questions.memoryTip: 先陈述否定事实 'Your father isn’t a teacher'，再抛出疑问 'is he?'
questions.word: Your father isn’t a teacher, is he?
questions.meaning: 你爸爸不是一名老师，是吗/对吗?

questions.exampleTranslation: 是的，他不是。

questions.explanation: 中考易错点！中文说'是的，他不是'，但在英语中，只要事实是'不是'，就必须用 No 开头，即 No, he isn't. 绝对不能出现 Yes 和 isn't 连用的情况。
questions.memoryTip: 英语很诚实：只要结果是'否'（不是老师），第一句话必须喊出 No！
questions.word: Your father isn’t a teacher, is he?

questions.meaning: n.行为/行动；表演; v.行为/行动;表演/扮演；充当
questions.example: She acted as a mother in the play.
questions.exampleTranslation: 她在剧中扮演一个母亲。

questions.question: 她在剧中扮演一个母亲。
options: She acted as a mother in the play.
Helping the old lady is an act of kindness.
She was more famous as a writer than as a singer.
He became internationally famous for his novels.
questions.correctAnswer: She acted as a mother in the play.
questions.explanation: acted as 表示扮演...的角色，in the play 是在戏剧中。
questions.memoryTip: act as = 扮演/充当，就像在舞台上演戏一样！
questions.meaning: n.行为/行动；表演; v.行为/行动;表演/扮演；充当
questions.example: She acted as a mother in the play.
questions.exampleTranslation: 她在剧中扮演一个母亲。

questions.question: Sharing your favorite toy with a friend is _an act of kindness_.
options: n.行为/行动；表演; v.行为/行动;表演/扮演；充当
因为…而有名/闻名；以…而有名/闻名
questions.explanation: kindness是善良，an act of kindness就是做了一件善良的事，即善行。
questions.memoryTip: act(行为) + kindness(善良) = 善行，做个善良的小天使！
questions.example: Helping the old lady is an act of kindness.
questions.exampleTranslation: 帮助那位老妇人是一种善行。

questions.question: 善行: ___
questions.explanation: 完全正确！善良的举动是最棒的。
questions.memoryTip: 一件(an) 行为(act) 关于(of) 善良(kindness) = 善行！
questions.example: Helping the old lady is an act of kindness.
questions.exampleTranslation: 帮助那位老妇人是一种善行。

questions.question: 帮助那位老妇人是一种善行。
options: Helping the old lady is an act of kindness.
She acted as a mother in the play.
He became internationally famous for his novels.
She was more famous as a writer than as a singer.
questions.correctAnswer: Helping the old lady is an act of kindness.
questions.explanation: Helping the old lady是帮助老妇人，is an act of kindness就是这是一种善行。
questions.memoryTip: 帮助别人(helping) 就是最棒的善行(act of kindness)！
questions.example: Helping the old lady is an act of kindness.
questions.exampleTranslation: 帮助那位老妇人是一种善行。

originalVocab.example: He became internationally famous for his novels.
originalVocab.exampleTranslation: 他因为他的小说享誉国际。

originalVocab.meaning: 因为…而有名/闻名；以…而有名/闻名
originalVocab.example: He became internationally famous for his novels.
originalVocab.exampleTranslation: 他因为他的小说享誉国际。

originalVocab.example: She was more famous as a writer than as a singer.
originalVocab.exampleTranslation: 她作为一名作家比作为一名歌手更有名/名声要大。

originalVocab.meaning: n.行为/行动；表演; v.行为/行动;表演/扮演；充当
originalVocab.example: She acted as a mother in the play.
originalVocab.exampleTranslation: 她在剧中扮演一个母亲。

originalVocab.example: Helping the old lady is an act of kindness.
originalVocab.exampleTranslation: 帮助那位老妇人是一种善行。

questions.question: The puppy loves to _bathe_ in the warm water.
questions.explanation: Great job! To bathe means to wash yourself in water.
questions.memoryTip: Bathe sounds like 'bath' - think of a fun bubble bath!

questions.question: 洗澡: ___
questions.explanation: Awesome! You spelled it perfectly.
questions.memoryTip: When you bathe, you have a 'B'ubble bath!

questions.question: It is _nearly_ time for us to go to the park!
questions.explanation: You nailed it! Nearly means almost or not quite there yet.
questions.memoryTip: Nearly sounds like 'near' - you are very near to the end!

questions.question: 几乎: ___
questions.explanation: Super! Nearly means it's almost happening.
questions.memoryTip: If you are nearly there, you are 'near' the goal.

questions.question: We are going on a fun trip during our school _holiday_.
questions.explanation: Correct! A holiday is a special day or time off to have fun.
questions.memoryTip: Holiday sounds like a 'holy' 'day' - a special happy day!

questions.question: 假日: ___
questions.explanation: Fantastic! You remembered the word for a fun day off.
questions.memoryTip: On a holiday, you get to play all day!

originalVocab.example: I bathe my dog outside when the weather is warm.
I have nearly finished reading my book.
We are going to the mountains for our summer holiday.

questions.question: I have a doctor's _appointment_ today at four o'clock.
questions.explanation: You got it! Appointment means a planned time to meet someone.
questions.memoryTip: Point to your watch: I have an appoint-ment to meet a friend!

questions.question: 预约: ___
questions.explanation: Great job! Remember that an appointment is when you set a time to see someone.
questions.memoryTip: Think of 'a-point-ment' as pointing to a specific time on a clock!

questions.question: It is _urgent_ to call for help when you see a fire.
questions.explanation: Excellent! Urgent means something needs your attention right now.
questions.memoryTip: Urgent sounds like 'urge' - like you need to act in a hurry!

questions.question: 紧急的: ___
questions.explanation: Spot on! If something is urgent, it's very important and needs action fast.
questions.memoryTip: U-R-gent... You Are gent(le) but you must hurry because it is urgent!

questions.question: The fire alarm rang and we had to leave the building _urgently_.
questions.explanation: Perfect! Urgently means you do something quickly because it is important.
questions.memoryTip: Just like 'urgent' but with an 'ly' to show how you are doing it!

questions.question: 紧急地: ___
questions.explanation: Correct! You are doing great with these tricky words.
questions.memoryTip: When you see 'ly', think of it as describing how you act in an emergency.

questions.question: I will play outside _till_ the sun goes down.
questions.explanation: Right! Till tells us when something ends.
questions.memoryTip: Till is a short way to say until!

questions.question: 直到: ___
questions.explanation: Perfect! Till is a great word to use when talking about time limits.
questions.memoryTip: Till sounds like 'tell' - tell me when you will stop!

questions.question: I _hope_ you have a wonderful day at school today!
questions.explanation: Exactly! You hope for something good to happen.
questions.memoryTip: When you hope, you look up (high) for good things to come!

questions.question: 希望: ___
questions.explanation: Wonderful! Keep practicing and you will remember these forever.
questions.memoryTip: H-O-P-E: Have Only Positive Expectations!

originalVocab.example: I have an appointment with the dentist today.
This is an urgent message, so please read it now.
The sick patient needs to see a doctor urgently.
Please wait here till I come back.
I hope we can go to the playground tomorrow.

questions.question: The model wears the latest _fashion_ on the runway.
questions.explanation: Fashion refers to the latest style in clothing and appearance!
questions.memoryTip: Fast fashion moves quickly, just like the latest trends.

questions.question: 时尚: ___
questions.explanation: Great job! Fashion is all about what is popular right now.
questions.memoryTip: Think of a 'fast-moving' style icon.

questions.question: I gave my friend a hug to _comfort_ her when she was sad.
questions.explanation: To comfort someone is to make them feel better when they are upset.
questions.memoryTip: Com-fort means coming to give strength and support.

questions.question: 安慰: ___
questions.explanation: You are doing great! Comforting a friend is a very kind thing to do.
questions.memoryTip: Imagine being in a warm, cozy blanket of support.

questions.question: This sofa is so _comfortable_ that I could nap on it all day!
questions.explanation: Comfortable describes something that feels soft and nice to sit or rest on.
questions.memoryTip: If it has 'comfort' inside, it makes you feel relaxed!

questions.question: 舒适的: ___
questions.explanation: Correct! When you feel relaxed in your bed, it is very comfortable.
questions.memoryTip: Come-for-table: Come sit at the table and relax!

questions.question: Are you _afraid_ of the dark at night?
questions.explanation: Afraid is how you feel when you are scared of something.
questions.memoryTip: A-fraid sounds like 'a frayed' (worn out) rope that makes you nervous!

questions.question: 害怕的: ___
questions.explanation: Fantastic! It is okay to be afraid sometimes, even big heroes get scared.
questions.memoryTip: Think of a ghost saying 'Aaaah!' and feeling afraid.

questions.question: My mom had to _appoint_ a time to see the dentist.
questions.explanation: To appoint is to set a specific time to meet or do something.
questions.memoryTip: Appoint starts like 'a point'—pick a point on the calendar!

questions.question: 预约: ___
questions.explanation: Amazing work! Making a plan is important to get things done.
questions.memoryTip: Think of making an 'appointment' by choosing a point in time.

originalVocab.example: She likes to dress in the latest fashion.
My mom gave me a warm hug to comfort me.
This large sofa is very comfortable to sit on.
The little boy is afraid of spiders.
We need to appoint a time for our meeting tomorrow.

questions.question: We need to _hurry_ to catch the school bus!
questions.explanation: Hurry means to move quickly or act fast because you are in a rush!
questions.memoryTip: Hurry sounds like 'hairy' - hurry up before you get hairy legs waiting!

questions.question: 赶快: ___
questions.explanation: Great job! Remember to use 'hurry' when you need to be fast.
questions.memoryTip: Hurry! Don't worry, just hurry!

questions.question: The rabbit ran _hurriedly_ back to its burrow.
questions.explanation: Hurriedly describes doing something in a big rush.
questions.memoryTip: It is 'hurry' with 'ly' at the end, like a quick little action.

questions.question: 匆忙地: ___
questions.explanation: Awesome! You spelled it right.
questions.memoryTip: Hurriedly ends in -ly, just like quickly!

questions.question: It is polite to _greet_ your teacher when you walk in.
questions.explanation: To greet someone is to say hello or welcome them.
questions.memoryTip: Greet sounds like 'great' - say a great hello!

questions.question: 问候: ___
questions.explanation: Perfect! Giving a greeting is a nice way to be a friend.
questions.memoryTip: G-r-e-e-t, meet and greet!

questions.question: A friendly _greeting_ makes everyone feel welcome.
questions.explanation: A greeting is the action of saying hello.
questions.memoryTip: Greeting is the noun version of the action greet.

questions.question: 问候: ___
questions.explanation: Fantastic! Keep up the good work.
questions.memoryTip: Greeting has 'ing' at the end, a friendly action!

questions.question: We walked a long _way_ to get to the park.
questions.explanation: Way refers to the path or direction you are traveling.
questions.memoryTip: Think of a 'way' as a road you walk on today!

questions.question: 道路: ___
questions.explanation: Brilliant! You found the right word.
questions.memoryTip: Way is a short word for a long path!

originalVocab.example: We must hurry so we are not late for school.
He hurriedly packed his bag and ran to the bus.
I always greet my teacher with a big smile.
Saying hello is a friendly greeting.
Can you show me the way to the library?

questions.question: My dad gives me some _pocket money_ every week to buy small treats.
questions.explanation: Great job! Pocket money is the small amount of money given for your own use.
questions.memoryTip: Keep the money in your pocket, that is your pocket money!

questions.question: 零花钱: ___
questions.explanation: Perfect! Pocket money is the fun money you get to save or spend.
questions.memoryTip: Small change in your pocket is your pocket money.

questions.question: Please repeat the _phrase_ after me: 'How are you?'.
questions.explanation: Correct! A phrase is a small group of words that work together.
questions.memoryTip: A phrase is just a 'fra-zy' little group of words!

questions.question: 短语: ___
questions.explanation: You're a star! Phrases are building blocks for sentences.
questions.memoryTip: Think of a 'phrase' as a 'phase' of a sentence.

questions.question: The turtle is very _slow_ when it walks.
questions.explanation: Exactly! Slow means it takes a long time to do something.
questions.memoryTip: S-l-o-w, like a snail dragging its shell!

questions.question: 缓慢的: ___
questions.explanation: Well done! Slow is the opposite of fast.
questions.memoryTip: Draw the word slow in very slow handwriting!

questions.question: The snail moved _slowly_ across the garden leaf.
questions.explanation: Nice work! Adding 'ly' often describes how something is done.
questions.memoryTip: Slow-ly, step by step, we get there!

questions.question: 缓慢地: ___
questions.explanation: Correct! You are doing great with these adverbs.
questions.memoryTip: Say 'slowly' as slowly as you can!

questions.question: After playing in the sun, I am very _thirsty_ and need water.
questions.explanation: Yes! Thirsty means your body needs a drink of water.
questions.memoryTip: When you are thirsty, you want a 'thirst'-quencher drink!

questions.question: 口渴的: ___
questions.explanation: Fantastic! Remember to drink water when you feel thirsty.
questions.memoryTip: The 'th' in thirsty sounds like a dry 'th-th-th' sound.

originalVocab.example: I keep my pocket money in a piggy bank.
Good morning is a very polite phrase.
The turtle is a very slow animal.
The little snail moves slowly across the leaf.
I felt very thirsty after running outside.

questions.question: This is the _last_ piece of cake on the plate.
questions.explanation: Great job! 'Last' means the one at the very end of a line or sequence.
questions.memoryTip: Last sounds like 'lost'—once it's the last one, it's gone!

questions.question: 最后的: ___
questions.explanation: Correct! You finished strong like the 'last' runner in a race!
questions.memoryTip: Think of the 'last' person standing in a line.

questions.question: The weather went through a _sudden change_ from sunny to rainy.
questions.explanation: Spot on! 'Sudden change' happens very quickly.
questions.memoryTip: Sudden starts with 'sud', like a splash of water that surprises you!

questions.question: 骤变: ___
questions.explanation: Well done! A 'sudden change' is like magic, switching in an instant.
questions.memoryTip: Combine 'sudden' (fast) and 'change' (different) to make 'sudden change'!

questions.question: The cat jumped _suddenly_ onto the table.
questions.explanation: Perfect! 'Suddenly' describes something that happens without warning.
questions.memoryTip: Suddenly starts with 'sud'—just like a sudden surprise!

questions.question: 突然地: ___
questions.explanation: Excellent! You are learning fast.
questions.memoryTip: Add -ly to sudden to describe how something happens suddenly.

questions.question: Only a _handful_ of cookies were left in the jar.
questions.explanation: Exactly! A handful is just enough to fit in your hand.
questions.memoryTip: Hand + full = A handful, literally what fits in your hand!

questions.question: 少数: ___
questions.explanation: Correct! You have a handful of brain power today!
questions.memoryTip: Imagine grabbing a handful of candies with one hand.

questions.question: I put the shiny rock in my _pocket_.
questions.explanation: Yes! A pocket is a tiny storage space on your clothes.
questions.memoryTip: Think of a 'pot' that holds small things in your clothes—a 'p-ocket'!

questions.question: 口袋: ___
questions.explanation: You got it! Keep those words in your memory pocket.
questions.memoryTip: Pocket starts with 'p' like 'put'—you put things in your pocket.

originalVocab.example: I was the last person to leave the classroom.
There was a sudden change in the weather and it rained.
The cat suddenly jumped onto the table.
Only a handful of people came to the park.
I found a shiny coin in my pocket.

questions.question: The muddy shoes made the room smell _awful_.
questions.explanation: When something is awful, it is really not good or pleasant, just like a bad smell!
questions.memoryTip: Awful sounds like 'all full' of bad things!

questions.question: 让人讨厌的: ___
questions.explanation: You did great! Awful means something is very unpleasant.
questions.memoryTip: Imagine an 'aw-full' monster that makes everyone say 'aww' in a bad way.

questions.question: I went to the park _once_ this week.
questions.explanation: Once means it happened one time.
questions.memoryTip: Once starts with 'o' like one!

questions.question: 一次: ___
questions.explanation: Perfect! Once is how we say one time.
questions.memoryTip: Think of 'once' upon a time, which is just one story start!

questions.question: I brushed my teeth _twice_ today, in the morning and at night.
questions.explanation: Twice means two times.
questions.memoryTip: Twice starts with 't' for two!

questions.question: 两次: ___
questions.explanation: Excellent! Twice is the special word for two times.
questions.memoryTip: Two, Twice... they both start with the same sound!

questions.question: I jumped _three times_ to win the game.
questions.explanation: Three times means exactly three separate actions.
questions.memoryTip: It has the word three right in it!

questions.question: 三次: ___
questions.explanation: You're doing amazing! That is exactly three times.
questions.memoryTip: Counting to three is easy, just add 'times' at the end!

questions.question: Please give me the right _answer_ to the puzzle.
questions.explanation: An answer is what you say when someone asks you something.
questions.memoryTip: An answer fixes your question!

questions.question: 回答: ___
questions.explanation: Well done! You provided the correct answer.
questions.memoryTip: A-n-s-w-e-r: A New Solution Will End Results!

originalVocab.example: The cold medicine tastes really awful.
I visit the library once a week.
Remember to brush your teeth twice a day.
I knocked on the door three times.
Please raise your hand if you know the answer.

questions.question: The train arrived at the _station_ on time.
questions.explanation: Great job! A station is a place where trains stop to pick up passengers.
questions.memoryTip: Think of a train 'staying' at the station.

questions.question: 车站: ___
questions.explanation: Correct! Station is the perfect word for a stop for trains.
questions.memoryTip: Remember that you 'stay' at the station while waiting!

questions.question: We went to the _airport_ to catch our flight to visit grandma.
questions.explanation: You got it! Airport is where planes land and take off.
questions.memoryTip: Air + port = a port for things that travel in the air!

questions.question: 机场: ___
questions.explanation: Amazing! Planes go to the airport.
questions.memoryTip: Remember it as a 'port' for 'air' planes.

questions.question: The _stationer_ sells pens and notebooks to the students.
questions.explanation: Excellent! A stationer is a person who sells stationary goods.
questions.memoryTip: A stationer is a person who stays at his shop to sell pens.

questions.question: 文具商: ___
questions.explanation: Well done! The shop owner is the stationer.
questions.memoryTip: It sounds like 'station', and he works in his station-ery shop!

questions.question: I bought some new _stationery_ for my first day of school.
questions.explanation: Perfect! Stationery includes pens, pencils, and paper.
questions.memoryTip: Stationery items stay on your desk when you write!

questions.question: 文具: ___
questions.explanation: Spot on! Stationery is essential for school.
questions.memoryTip: Ends with 'ery' like 'everything' you need to write!

questions.question: There are _hundred_ pages in this thick storybook.
questions.explanation: You're a star! Hundred is the number 100.
questions.memoryTip: A hundred is a big number that starts with 'hun' like hungry!

questions.question: 一百: ___
questions.explanation: Fantastic! You know your numbers well.
questions.memoryTip: Think of a 'hundred' as a big group of 10 tens!

originalVocab.example: We waited for our train at the station.
We saw huge airplanes landing at the airport.
I bought a new blue pen from the stationer.
My pencil case is filled with cute stationery.
There are one hundred cents in a dollar.

questions.question: I was so _lucky_ to find a shiny coin on the sidewalk!
questions.explanation: Being lucky means having good things happen to you unexpectedly!
questions.memoryTip: Lucky people feel like they found a 'luck' pot of gold!

questions.question: 幸运的: ___
questions.explanation: You got it! Lucky is the word for when good fortune shines on you.
questions.memoryTip: Think of a four-leaf clover, which is very lucky!

questions.question: The fast rabbit joined the _race_ against the slow turtle.
questions.explanation: A race is a competition to see who can get to the finish line first!
questions.memoryTip: In a race, everyone runs fast to reach the place (race) first.

questions.question: 赛跑: ___
questions.explanation: Great job! A race is a fun way to test your speed.
questions.memoryTip: R-A-C-E: Run Around Constantly Exercising!

questions.question: The _winner_ of the game held the trophy high in the air.
questions.explanation: The winner is the person who comes in first place.
questions.memoryTip: Winners win! Start with a W for Winner and Win!

questions.question: 赢家: ___
questions.explanation: Awesome! You identified the person who wins the game.
questions.memoryTip: A winner is someone who finishes in the 'win' position.

questions.question: I felt so _excited_ when my friend arrived at my birthday party!
questions.explanation: Excited means you are full of energy and very happy about something!
questions.memoryTip: Ex-cited! Like you want to jump up and out of your chair!

questions.question: 激动的: ___
questions.explanation: Spot on! Excited describes that jumpy, happy feeling.
questions.memoryTip: When you are excited, your heart beats like a drum!

questions.question: Watching the colorful fireworks was an _exciting_ experience.
questions.explanation: Exciting is something that causes that happy, energetic feeling in you!
questions.memoryTip: If something makes you 'excite', it is 'exciting'!

questions.question: 令人激动的: ___
questions.explanation: Wonderful! Exciting describes the thing that makes you feel happy.
questions.memoryTip: It ends in -ing because it keeps you going and interested!

originalVocab.example: I am so lucky to have such good friends.
The boys ran very fast in the race.
The winner of the game gets a gold medal.
I am so excited to visit the zoo tomorrow.
Riding the big roller coaster is very exciting.

questions.question: The United States is a large _nation_.
questions.explanation: Great job! A nation is a country, which is a group of people living under one government.
questions.memoryTip: Think of the nation as a big group of friends living in one big home called a country.

questions.question: 国家: ___
questions.explanation: You got it! Nation starts with 'nat' like nature, representing all the people in the country.
questions.memoryTip: Nat-ion has 'nat' in it, just like the whole country is a big family.

questions.question: He sang the _national_ anthem loudly.
questions.explanation: Correct! National describes things that belong to or represent the whole country.
questions.memoryTip: National sounds like 'nation-al', meaning it belongs to the nation!

questions.question: 国家的: ___
questions.explanation: Perfect! When something is national, it is official for the whole country.
questions.memoryTip: National is just 'nation' plus 'al' to show it describes something of the country.

questions.question: What is your _nationality_?
questions.explanation: Spot on! Nationality tells us which country you are a citizen of.
questions.memoryTip: Nationality tells the world which nation you call home!

questions.question: 国籍: ___
questions.explanation: Excellent! Nationality connects you to your country.
questions.memoryTip: It's a long word for a big question: 'What is your nationality?'

questions.question: Flowers grow in the _natural_ garden.
questions.explanation: Well done! Natural means things that come from nature, not made by machines.
questions.memoryTip: Natural things are just the way nature made them!

questions.question: 自然的: ___
questions.explanation: You are doing great! Natural is easy to spot in forests and parks.
questions.memoryTip: Think of nature, the birds and trees—that is all very natural.

questions.question: I had good _luck_ finding the treasure.
questions.explanation: Fantastic! Luck is when something good happens by surprise.
questions.memoryTip: Having good luck is like hitting a winning button!

questions.question: 运气: ___
questions.explanation: Awesome work! Luck is a short, fun word for when things go your way.
questions.memoryTip: Luck rhymes with 'duck'—imagine a lucky duck finding a coin!

originalVocab.example: Our nation has a very long history.
We stood up to sing the national anthem.
He asked me what my nationality is.
The park is filled with natural beauty.
I wish you good luck on your math test.

questions.question: I will _attend_ the magic school party tomorrow.
questions.explanation: Great job! Attend means to show up or be present at an event.
questions.memoryTip: A-T-T-E-N-D: At the event and need to be there!

questions.question: 出席: ___
questions.explanation: You nailed it! Attend is the word for going to a gathering.
questions.memoryTip: A-T-T-E-N-D sounds like a-ten-dances, like counting who is there!

questions.question: We love to play outside on _weekends_.
questions.explanation: Awesome! Weekends are the best time for fun after the school week.
questions.memoryTip: Week-ends: the ends of the week!

questions.question: 周末: ___
questions.explanation: Spot on! Weekends are Saturday and Sunday.
questions.memoryTip: Think of the week finishing up.

questions.question: I have school during the _weekdays_.
questions.explanation: Well done! Weekdays are the busy days when we have school.
questions.memoryTip: Week-days: the days in the middle of the week.

questions.question: 工作日: ___
questions.explanation: Superb! Weekdays are the days between Monday and Friday.
questions.memoryTip: These are the days that 'work' for school and jobs.

questions.question: _Monday_ is the first day of our school week.
questions.explanation: Excellent! Monday starts our busy week.
questions.memoryTip: Mon-day: 'Mon' sounds like the Moon, shining on the first day!

questions.question: 周一: ___
questions.explanation: You are a star! Monday is the day after the weekend.
questions.memoryTip: Monday is for starting fresh!

questions.question: I like to _spend_ time with my friends.
questions.explanation: Fantastic! Spend is used when we use time or money.
questions.memoryTip: Spend... lend your time or money!

questions.question: 度过/花费: ___
questions.explanation: Correct! You did a wonderful job.
questions.memoryTip: S-P-E-N-D, think of sending your time on something fun.

originalVocab.example: I will attend my friend's birthday party on Saturday.
I do not go to school on the weekends.
We have classes early on weekdays.
Monday is the first day of the school week.
I spend my pocket money on comic books.

questions.question: The _greengrocer_ sells fresh apples and carrots for us to eat.
questions.explanation: Correct! A greengrocer is someone who brings green vegetables to your table.
questions.memoryTip: Green for vegetables, grocer for the shop!

questions.question: 蔬菜水果零售商: ___
questions.explanation: Great job! A greengrocer is the person you visit for healthy greens.
questions.memoryTip: Think of a 'green' grocer who loves veggies!

questions.question: The _baker_ makes yummy bread and cakes every morning.
questions.explanation: Spot on! The baker is the hero who makes our favorite breakfast toast.
questions.memoryTip: Bake rhymes with cake, and the baker loves to make it!

questions.question: 面包师: ___
questions.explanation: You are amazing! A baker is a master of oven treats.
questions.memoryTip: You 'bake' in the oven, so the person doing it is a baker.

questions.question: I need to visit the _hairdresser_ to trim my hair so it looks neat.
questions.explanation: Well done! The hairdresser helps keep our hair style looking great.
questions.memoryTip: Hair-dresser: someone who dresses up your hair!

questions.question: 理发师: ___
questions.explanation: Perfect! A hairdresser is the one who keeps your haircut looking cool.
questions.memoryTip: Think of someone fixing your 'hair' and 'dressing' it up.

questions.question: We went to the _dairy_ to buy some fresh milk and cheese.
questions.explanation: You got it! A dairy is the best place to find yummy milk and yogurt.
questions.memoryTip: Dairy cows give milk, and the dairy shop keeps it cold for you!

questions.question: 乳制品店: ___
questions.explanation: Awesome work! Dairy products like milk and cheese come from the dairy.
questions.memoryTip: Milk comes from cows at the dairy farm!

questions.question: People go to the _church_ on Sundays to sing and pray together.
questions.explanation: Exactly! The church is a quiet, special place for many people.
questions.memoryTip: Church starts with C, just like the peace and calm you feel there.

questions.question: 教堂: ___
questions.explanation: Super! A church is a wonderful place for community and song.
questions.memoryTip: Think of the bells ringing in the tall church tower.

originalVocab.example: We bought fresh apples from the greengrocer.
The baker makes delicious cakes and bread.
The hairdresser gave me a nice haircut.
We buy fresh milk and cheese from the dairy.
People sing hymns together in the church.

questions.question: The children were very _noisy_ in the playground.
questions.explanation: That's right! Noisy means making a lot of sound, just like a busy playground!
questions.memoryTip: Noisy has the word 'no' in it—when it is noisy, you have to say 'no' to keep things quiet.

questions.question: 吵闹的: ___
questions.explanation: Great job! Noisy is the perfect word to describe a loud environment.
questions.memoryTip: Think of a 'no-see' monster being loud and noisy!

questions.question: Be careful not to _fall_ down the stairs!
questions.explanation: Correct! Fall means to drop down towards the ground.
questions.memoryTip: When you fall, you feel like you are 'following' the ground.

questions.question: 跌落: ___
questions.explanation: Excellent! You remembered that fall is when something drops down.
questions.memoryTip: Think of leaves falling from trees in autumn, which is also called Fall!

questions.question: I can't open the door without my _key_.
questions.explanation: Spot on! A key is what we use to unlock doors.
questions.memoryTip: A key opens the door to your 'key-dom' (kingdom)!

questions.question: 钥匙: ___
questions.explanation: Wonderful! You know that a key is the tool used for locks.
questions.memoryTip: Key rhymes with see—you use a key so you can see what is inside!

questions.question: Do you _enjoy_ eating ice cream?
questions.explanation: Yes! Enjoy means you really like doing something.
questions.memoryTip: Enjoy starts with 'en', think of the joy you feel inside!

questions.question: 喜欢: ___
questions.explanation: Perfect! Enjoying something means it makes you feel happy.
questions.memoryTip: When you are enjoying something, you are full of joy.

questions.question: The _grocer_ sells fresh apples and carrots.
questions.explanation: Correct! A grocer is a person who sells food items.
questions.memoryTip: A grocer sells 'groceries'—think of food growing in the garden.

questions.question: 食品杂货商: ___
questions.explanation: Fantastic! A grocer is the friendly person who runs the food store.
questions.memoryTip: Grocer sounds like grow-sir—the person who helps food grow and be sold!

originalVocab.example: The playground is very noisy during recess.
Be careful not to fall down the stairs.
I need the key to open the front door.
I really enjoy reading comic books.
The grocer sells rice, beans, and other foods.

questions.question: The _rich people_ in the story lived in a giant gold castle.
questions.explanation: Great job! Rich people have a lot of money and belongings.
questions.memoryTip: Rich people like to be 'reach'ing for more money!

questions.question: 富人: ___
questions.explanation: Correct! Rich people start with 'rich' which means having plenty.
questions.memoryTip: Remember, the 'rich' get richer!

questions.question: It is sad that the _poor people_ do not have enough food to eat.
questions.explanation: You got it! Poor people have very little money.
questions.memoryTip: Poor rhymes with 'door'—imagine a poor person knocking on a door for help.

questions.question: 穷人: ___
questions.explanation: Perfect! Poor people are those who don't have much money.
questions.memoryTip: Poor starts with 'P', like 'please help'.

questions.question: Can you _match_ your left sock with your right sock?
questions.explanation: Well done! To match means to find two things that look the same.
questions.memoryTip: Match sounds like 'catch'—catch the pair that belongs together!

questions.question: 匹配: ___
questions.explanation: Correct! Matching is like playing a fun game of finding pairs.
questions.memoryTip: Think of a 'match' stick—two of them together look identical.

questions.question: Don't let the tower _lean_ too far or it will fall down!
questions.explanation: Excellent! When something leans, it is not standing straight.
questions.memoryTip: Leaning starts with 'L', like the Leaning Tower of Pisa.

questions.question: 倾斜: ___
questions.explanation: Awesome! Lean means to tilt to one side.
questions.memoryTip: If you are tired, you might lean on a wall to support yourself.

questions.question: After running fast, you should sit down and _rest_ for a while.
questions.explanation: Correct! Rest helps your body feel strong again.
questions.memoryTip: Rest sounds like 'best'—you feel your best after a good rest.

questions.question: 休息: ___
questions.explanation: You're a superstar! Rest is what we do when we are sleepy.
questions.memoryTip: Imagine resting your head on a soft pillow.

originalVocab.example: The rich people lived in a very large castle.
We should always try to help poor people.
Try to match the red shoes with the red bag.
Do not lean too far out of the window.
Let us take a short rest after running.

questions.question: The flowers _smell_ so sweet!
questions.explanation: Great job! Smell refers to using your nose to sense a scent.
questions.memoryTip: Smell sounds like 'smoke' - you can smell smoke with your nose!

questions.question: 闻起来: ___
questions.explanation: Correct! You use your nose to smell flowers.
questions.memoryTip: Think of smelling a fresh flower.

questions.question: Please do not _touch_ the painting.
questions.explanation: Perfect! Touch means to make contact with your hand or skin.
questions.memoryTip: Touch starts with T, think of Tapping with your finger.

questions.question: 触摸: ___
questions.explanation: Well done! You touch things to feel their texture.
questions.memoryTip: T for Touch, T for Tap!

questions.question: The sick puppy went to see the _vet_.
questions.explanation: Correct! A vet is a doctor who helps sick animals.
questions.memoryTip: Vet is short for veterinary. Think of a 'V' for Very kind to pets.

questions.question: 兽医: ___
questions.explanation: You're doing amazing! The vet looks after our furry friends.
questions.memoryTip: Vets visit pets!

questions.question: Open your _mouth_ and say ahhh.
questions.explanation: Excellent! Your mouth is what you use to eat and talk.
questions.memoryTip: Mouth makes 'mmm' sounds when you eat something tasty!

questions.question: 嘴巴: ___
questions.explanation: Correct! Use your mouth to smile and eat.
questions.memoryTip: Mouth has 'M' like 'mummy' who feeds you.

questions.question: Stick out your _tongue_!
questions.explanation: Well done! The tongue helps you taste food.
questions.memoryTip: Tongue starts with 'T' and you 'taste' with it.

questions.question: 舌头: ___
questions.explanation: You've got it! Your tongue helps you taste delicious treats.
questions.memoryTip: Tongue is a long word for a long muscle in your mouth!

originalVocab.example: The baked cookies smell wonderful.
Do not touch the stove when it is hot.
We took our sick cat to the vet.
Open your mouth wide for the dentist.
The dog licked my hand with its tongue.

questions.question: We had a long _conversation_ about our favorite superheroes.
questions.explanation: Great job! A conversation is when two or more people talk together.
questions.memoryTip: Con-ver-sa-tion: Imagine two people con-necting their voices together!

questions.question: 会谈: ___
questions.explanation: Spot on! Conversation is a back-and-forth talk.
questions.memoryTip: Think of a 'con' (together) 'verse' (speech) between friends.

questions.question: I _feel_ so happy today because the sun is shining!
questions.explanation: Exactly! Feel describes the emotions or sensations you have inside.
questions.memoryTip: When you feel happy, your heart grows!

questions.question: 感觉: ___
questions.explanation: You've got it! You feel things with your heart and your hands.
questions.memoryTip: Feel sounds like 'fill'—let your feelings fill your heart!

questions.question: Please _look_ at the beautiful butterfly on the flower.
questions.explanation: Correct! When you look, you use your eyes to see something.
questions.memoryTip: The two 'o's in 'look' look just like two wide-open eyes!

questions.question: 看: ___
questions.explanation: Wonderful! You use your eyes to look at the world.
questions.memoryTip: Remember, the 'oo' in look are like two eyes staring at the book!

questions.question: I want to _taste_ the yummy strawberry cake.
questions.explanation: Yes! You taste food with your tongue to see if it is yummy.
questions.memoryTip: Taste starts with 'T'—just like your tongue!

questions.question: 尝: ___
questions.explanation: Excellent! Using your tongue to test food is to taste it.
questions.memoryTip: T-A-S-T-E: Tongue Always Senses The Eating!

questions.question: That song does _sound_ very cheerful!
questions.explanation: Well done! When you hear music, it has a sound.
questions.memoryTip: When you hear something, it makes a sound in your ears!

questions.question: 听起来: ___
questions.explanation: Correct! Things that you hear have a sound.
questions.memoryTip: Sound starts with 'S'—like the soft sound of a whisper!

originalVocab.example: My teacher had a long conversation with my parents.
I feel happy when I play with my dog.
Look at that colorful bird in the tree.
This sweet strawberry tastes very good.
The bells sound very loud from here.

questions.question: The _northern_ wind feels very cold today.
questions.explanation: Northern refers to the direction towards the North, which is usually quite chilly!
questions.memoryTip: Think of North as the top of the map, always pointing up!

questions.question: 北方的: ___
questions.explanation: Great job! Northern describes things coming from or located in the north.
questions.memoryTip: North + ern = Northern direction!

questions.question: I woke up early to see the beautiful _sunrise_.
questions.explanation: Sunrise is when the sun first appears in the morning sky.
questions.memoryTip: Sun + rise: The sun rises up in the morning!

questions.question: 日出: ___
questions.explanation: Spot on! The sun rising makes the sky look colorful.
questions.memoryTip: See the sun 'rise' to start the day.

questions.question: The sky turned orange and pink at _sunset_.
questions.explanation: Sunset is when the sun goes down and the day turns into night.
questions.memoryTip: Sun + set: The sun sets down to rest.

questions.question: 日落: ___
questions.explanation: Perfect! Sunset is the time to watch the sun disappear behind the horizon.
questions.memoryTip: Sun 'sets' like a toy on a shelf at night.

questions.question: This magic book is very _interesting_ to read.
questions.explanation: Interesting means something captures your attention because it is exciting!
questions.memoryTip: In-ter-est-ing: If it keeps you in it, it's interesting!

questions.question: 有趣的: ___
questions.explanation: Correct! When you find something fun, you can say it is interesting.
questions.memoryTip: It enters (inter) your mind because it is exciting!

questions.question: I am _interested_ in learning about space.
questions.explanation: Interested is how you feel when you really want to know more about something.
questions.memoryTip: Interested describes the person's feeling about a topic.

questions.question: 感兴趣的: ___
questions.explanation: Fantastic! You are interested because you love learning new things.
questions.memoryTip: I am -ed (interested) because I love it!

originalVocab.example: Polar bears live in the northern regions.
We woke up early to watch the beautiful sunrise.
The sky turned pink and orange at sunset.
This book about space is very interesting.
I am interested in learning how to play the piano.

questions.question: The sun sets in the _west_ every evening.
questions.explanation: Great job! The sun goes down in the west, so we call that direction the west!
questions.memoryTip: W is for West, think of it as Where the sun goes to sleep.

questions.question: 西: ___
questions.explanation: Spot on! West is the direction where the sun sets.
questions.memoryTip: W is for West, think of it as Where the sun goes to sleep.

questions.question: Polar bears live in the cold _north_ pole.
questions.explanation: Correct! The North Pole is very cold and way up at the top of the map.
questions.memoryTip: N is for North, think of 'No' freezing snow at the top!

questions.question: 北: ___
questions.explanation: You got it! North is the direction pointing towards the very top of the globe.
questions.memoryTip: N is for North, think of 'No' freezing snow at the top!

questions.question: The sunrise starts on the _eastern_ side of the sky.
questions.explanation: Well done! Anything that comes from or is related to the east is called eastern.
questions.memoryTip: Eastern starts with E for the Early morning sunrise!

questions.question: 东方的: ___
questions.explanation: Perfect! Eastern describes things located in the direction of the sunrise.
questions.memoryTip: Eastern starts with E for the Early morning sunrise!

questions.question: Many birds fly to _southern_ countries to stay warm in winter.
questions.explanation: Super! Southern describes places found towards the warm south.
questions.memoryTip: Southern sounds like 'South', where it is Sunny and warm!

questions.question: 南方的: ___
questions.explanation: Amazing work! Southern means relating to the south.
questions.memoryTip: Southern sounds like 'South', where it is Sunny and warm!

questions.question: We enjoy eating delicious _western_ food like pasta and pizza.
questions.explanation: Fantastic! Western refers to cultures or things from the west.
questions.memoryTip: Western is just 'West' plus 'ern', meaning it belongs in the west!

questions.question: 西方的: ___
questions.explanation: You're a star! Western is the word we use for things from the west.
questions.memoryTip: Western is just 'West' plus 'ern', meaning it belongs in the west!

originalVocab.example: The sun sets in the west every evening.
The compass needle points to the north.
We traveled to the eastern part of the country.
Penguins live in the southern part of the world.
I like reading stories about western cowboys.

questions.question: I _sometimes_ eat ice cream for breakfast.
questions.explanation: Great job! Sometimes means it happens every now and then!
questions.memoryTip: Some + times: some of the times, but not all!

questions.question: 有时: ___
questions.explanation: You nailed it! Sometimes is the word for '有时'.
questions.memoryTip: Sometimes is just a group of 'some' 'times'.

questions.question: He _hardly_ studies, so he does not know the answer.
questions.explanation: Spot on! Hardly means almost not at all.
questions.memoryTip: Hardly starts with Hard: if you hardly work, it is hard to get good grades.

questions.question: 几乎不: ___
questions.explanation: Excellent! Hardly means it rarely happens.
questions.memoryTip: It is 'hard' to see him because he hardly ever goes out.

questions.question: I _never_ touch the hot stove.
questions.explanation: Correct! Never means not at any time.
questions.memoryTip: Never starts with N, like No, never!

questions.question: 从不: ___
questions.explanation: You are doing great! Never means not ever.
questions.memoryTip: Think of 'Never ever'!

questions.question: The sun rises in the _east_.
questions.explanation: Brilliant! The sun always rises in the east.
questions.memoryTip: E for East, where the sun Enters the sky.

questions.question: 东: ___
questions.explanation: Correct! You know your directions well.
questions.memoryTip: East is where the sun wakes up!

questions.question: Birds fly to the _south_ in the winter to stay warm.
questions.explanation: Awesome! South is the opposite of North.
questions.memoryTip: S for South, S for Sunny, because it is usually warmer there.

questions.question: 南: ___
questions.explanation: Way to go! You remembered south perfectly.
questions.memoryTip: South starts with S for Sun.

originalVocab.example: Sometimes I help my dad wash the car.
I am so full that I can hardly eat another bite.
I never forget to do my homework.
The sun always rises in the east.
Many birds fly south for the winter.

questions.question: It is very _cold_ in winter.
questions.explanation: 冬天非常冷哦，cold就是冷的意思！
questions.memoryTip: 想像你在冰雪里发抖，C-O-L-D就是冷的！

questions.question: 冷的: ___
questions.explanation: 太棒了！冷的英文是cold。
questions.memoryTip: 想像你在冰雪里发抖，C-O-L-D就是冷的！

questions.question: The autumn breeze feels nice and _cool_.
questions.explanation: 秋风吹来凉凉的，cool表示凉爽的哦！
questions.memoryTip: Cool里面有两个o，像两片酷酷的太阳眼镜，戴上感觉很凉爽！

questions.question: 凉爽的: ___
questions.explanation: 拼写正确！cool就是凉爽的意思。
questions.memoryTip: Cool里面有两个o，像两片酷酷的太阳眼镜，戴上感觉很凉爽！

questions.question: I _always_ brush my teeth before bed.
questions.explanation: 每天都要刷牙哦！always表示总是、一直。
questions.memoryTip: All(全部)加ways(路)，所有的路都一样，说明你总是这样做！

questions.question: 总是: ___
questions.explanation: 真棒！always用来表示百分之百发生的事情：总是。
questions.memoryTip: All(全部)加ways(路)，所有的路都一样，说明你总是这样做！

questions.question: She _usually_ drinks milk for breakfast.
questions.explanation: 她一般情况下都喝牛奶，usually就是通常的意思。
questions.memoryTip: Usually读起来像由着理，通常我们做事都是由着理智来的！

questions.question: 通常: ___
questions.explanation: 完全正确！usually表示通常发生的事情。
questions.memoryTip: Usually读起来像由着理，通常我们做事都是由着理智来的！

questions.question: We _often_ play football in the park.
questions.explanation: 我们在公园里经常踢足球，often是经常的意思哦！
questions.memoryTip: Often里的t不发音！读作奥芬，像一阵经常吹来的风！

questions.question: 经常: ___
questions.explanation: 太聪明了！经常的英文就是often。
questions.memoryTip: Often里的t不发音！读作奥芬，像一阵经常吹来的风！

originalVocab.example: Put on your thick coat because it is cold outside.
The evening breeze feels very cool.
I always brush my teeth before going to bed.
I usually wake up at seven in the morning.
We often visit my grandparents on the weekend.

questions.question: We can see the bright blue sky on a _sunny_ day.
questions.explanation: Great job! Sunny means the sun is shining brightly in the sky.
questions.memoryTip: Sunny comes from the word 'Sun' (太阳). Just add an extra 'n' and 'y'!

questions.question: 晴朗的: ___
questions.explanation: Awesome! A sunny day is perfect for playing outside.
questions.memoryTip: Remember the two 'n's in sunny, like two warm sun rays!

questions.question: It is hard to see the road because it is very _foggy_.
questions.explanation: You got it! Foggy means there is thick fog making it hard to see far away.
questions.memoryTip: Foggy starts with F, like a floating white cloud near the ground!

questions.question: 有雾的: ___
questions.explanation: Super! Foggy days feel like you are walking through the clouds.
questions.memoryTip: Fog + g + y! The two g's look like cool goggles you need to see in the fog!

questions.question: The wind is blowing hard and it is very _stormy_.
questions.explanation: Correct! Stormy weather means strong winds, rain, and maybe lightning!
questions.memoryTip: Stormy starts with S, like the scary sound of strong wind!

questions.question: 有暴风雨的: ___
questions.explanation: Brilliant! It is best to stay cozy inside when it is stormy.
questions.memoryTip: Think of a Storm throwing things around in a Y shape! Storm + y = stormy!

questions.question: Spring brings nice and _warm_ weather.
questions.explanation: Perfect! Warm means it feels nice, not too hot and not too cold.
questions.memoryTip: Warm sounds like the English word 'worm' (虫), and worms love warm soil!

questions.question: 温暖的: ___
questions.explanation: Yay! Warm weather is great for planting beautiful flowers.
questions.memoryTip: W-A-R-M: Wrap Arms Round Me to feel warm!

questions.question: Be careful not to touch the stove, it is very _hot_!
questions.explanation: Excellent! Hot means having a very high temperature.
questions.memoryTip: Hot is short and sweet! Just 3 letters like breathing out hot air: H-O-T.

questions.question: 热的: ___
questions.explanation: Fantastic! We love eating cool ice cream on a hot day.
questions.memoryTip: H is for Heat, O is like the bright round sun, and T is the thermometer going up!

originalVocab.example: Let us go outside and play on this sunny day.
It is hard to see the road when it is foggy.
We stayed inside the house during the stormy weather.
The sun feels nice and warm on my face.
Be careful, the soup is very hot.

questions.question: We had a _pleasant_ picnic in the park.
questions.explanation: Great job! Pleasant means nice and enjoyable, like a fun picnic on a beautiful day.
questions.memoryTip: Please (请) 加上 ant (蚂蚁)，请蚂蚁吃糖是令人愉快的！

questions.question: 令人愉快的: ___
questions.explanation: Awesome! You remembered how to spell pleasant.
questions.memoryTip: Please (请) 加上 ant (蚂蚁)，请蚂蚁吃糖是令人愉快的！

questions.question: Hold onto your hat! It is very _windy_ outside!
questions.explanation: Spot on! When it is windy, the wind blows hard and can make things fly away.
questions.memoryTip: Wind (风) 加上 y 变成形容词，大风呼呼吹，windy就是有风的！

questions.question: 有风的: ___
questions.explanation: Fantastic! You spelled windy perfectly.
questions.memoryTip: Wind (风) 加上 y 变成形容词，大风呼呼吹，windy就是有风的！

questions.question: The sky is _cloudy_, so we cannot see the sun today.
questions.explanation: Correct! Cloudy means the sky is covered with fluffy clouds.
questions.memoryTip: Cloud (云) 加上 y，天上满是白云，cloudy就是多云的！

questions.question: 多云的: ___
questions.explanation: Wonderful! You knew that cloudy comes from the word cloud.
questions.memoryTip: Cloud (云) 加上 y，天上满是白云，cloudy就是多云的！

questions.question: Do not forget to bring your umbrella on a _rainy_ day.
questions.explanation: Excellent! Rainy means water is falling from the sky, so you need an umbrella.
questions.memoryTip: Rain (雨) 加上 y，滴答滴答，rainy就是下雨的！

questions.question: 下雨的: ___
questions.explanation: Super! Rainy is rain with a y at the end.
questions.memoryTip: Rain (雨) 加上 y，滴答滴答，rainy就是下雨的！

questions.question: We can build a funny snowman in the _snowy_ yard.
questions.explanation: Brilliant! Snowy days are perfect for making snowmen and having snowball fights.
questions.memoryTip: Snow (雪) 加上 y，雪花飘飘，snowy就是下雪的！

questions.question: 下雪的: ___
questions.explanation: Way to go! You are a superstar speller today.
questions.memoryTip: Snow (雪) 加上 y，雪花飘飘，snowy就是下雪的！

originalVocab.example: We had a pleasant walk in the park.
It is a windy day, so let us fly a kite.
The sky is cloudy and grey today.
I need my boots and umbrella on a rainy day.
The mountains look beautiful on a snowy morning.

questions.question: The leaves turn yellow and red in _autumn_.
questions.explanation: Autumn means the season before winter, when leaves fall!
questions.memoryTip: Au-tumn starts like 'Aw', looking at the awesome leaves falling!

questions.question: 秋: ___
questions.explanation: You got it! Autumn is the season of falling leaves.
questions.memoryTip: Au-tumn starts like 'Aw', looking at the awesome leaves falling!

questions.question: I love to build a snowman in _winter_.
questions.explanation: Winter is the coldest season when we get snow!
questions.memoryTip: Winter has 'win' in it. You win a snowball fight in winter!

questions.question: 冬: ___
questions.explanation: Great job! Winter is the snowy season.
questions.memoryTip: Winter has 'win' in it. You win a snowball fight in winter!

questions.question: He bought a nice gift to _please_ his mom.
questions.explanation: Here, please is an action word that means to make someone happy or satisfied.
questions.memoryTip: If you say 'please' when asking for something, you 'please' (使满意) your parents!

questions.question: 使满意: ___
questions.explanation: Awesome! Please is a magic word, and as a verb, it means to make someone happy.
questions.memoryTip: If you say 'please' when asking for something, you 'please' (使满意) your parents!

questions.question: The teacher was very _pleased_ with my good grades.
questions.explanation: Pleased describes a feeling of being happy or satisfied about something.
questions.memoryTip: When you are pleased, you are smiling like the 'ea' in pleased!

questions.question: 高兴的: ___
questions.explanation: Spot on! Pleased means you feel happy and glad.
questions.memoryTip: When you are pleased, you are smiling like the 'ea' in pleased!

questions.question: The soft music is very _pleasing_ to my ears.
questions.explanation: Pleasing describes something that gives you pleasure or satisfaction.
questions.memoryTip: Something pleas-ING is satisfy-ING!

questions.question: 令人满意的: ___
questions.explanation: Fantastic! Pleasing describes things that make us feel good.
questions.memoryTip: Something pleas-ING is satisfy-ING!

originalVocab.example: The leaves fall from the trees in autumn.
We can build a snowman in the winter.
I cleaned my room to please my mother.
I am very pleased with my good grades.
The piano music is very pleasing to hear.

questions.question: We pick pumpkins in _October_.
questions.explanation: October is the tenth month of the year!
questions.memoryTip: October starts with an O, like a big Orange pumpkin!

questions.question: 十月: ___
questions.explanation: Great job! October is month number 10.
